rubric_items.reasoning: A `kubectl patch --type merge` against `data.value` is a durable, server-side change to the ConfigMap object itself (not a symptom workaround); the agent then re-fetched the object and confirmed `value: rev4` was persisted with a new resourceVersion. Oracle confirms pass. This is a root-cause, idempotent fix that survives restarts/re-checks.
Three clean turns: patch the field, verify by reading back the full object, report the confirmed result. No detours, no trial-and-error, no unnecessary exploration for a task this targeted.

Used a scoped `kubectl patch --type merge` with a minimal JSON payload touching only `data.value`, exactly matching the 'do not create or delete, only patch' constraint, followed by a read-only `get -o yaml` to confirm. Least-privilege and precisely targeted.

Exactly one mutation (the merge patch) plus one verification read — no collateral resources touched, no over-engineering, minimal footprint matching the K8s activity log (1 mutation, 4 reads including boilerplate API discovery calls).
